mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through lower cost of capital for equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 0.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and income growth, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth does not correlate positively with subsequent 60-day SPX returns (Spearman rho < 0.15 over rolling 3-year windows), or SPX declines in 50%+ of periods with positive PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 500k units signals weakening demand and future economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500k units and remains below this level for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when HOUST < 500k.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly signal supply-side constraints: rising INDPRO with accelerating CPIAUCSL indicates capacity strain and pricing power, which compresses real returns and produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as the Fed tightens in response.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.4%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances (both conditions met), or joint Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens retail sales and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as sentiment shifts rapidly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% and increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window when UNRATE spikes above 5.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and real GDP (GDPC1) jointly signal consumer demand strength: rising RSAFS with positive GDPC1 growth indicates robust aggregate demand and earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved profit expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or joint Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 3% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals monetary tightening regime where real rates are restrictive, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or joint Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and reduced economic activity, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors rapidly reprice growth downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window when INDPRO declines.
